compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Git subprocess calls are scattered across ~8 reconciler modules with no central adapter, while Jira access IS centralized in `acli`. This asymmetry means git invocation policy (CWD, `-C`, error handling, timeouts) varies per call site — and it is the root of the F14 CWD bug. (SC5+SC6+SC7 — verified.)

## What
Introduce one `git_adapter` module (mirroring `acli`) exposing the git ops actually used (verify_commit, log_format, rev-parse, add/commit/push, ref-lock ops), each taking an explicit `repo_root` and passing `-C`. Migrate the ~8 modules to it. Route the inline binding-store commit-back through it and lift hardcoded paths to constants. Pulling git out of `_attestation.py` also resolves SC7 (leaving that module to Jira-settings + attestation policy).

Citations: SC6 sites — `__main__.py`, `_advisory_lock.py`, `_attestation.py:121,135`, `_concurrency.py`, `_ref_lock.py`, `apply_outbound.py:62-68`, `reconcile.py`; SC5 inline commit-back — `reconcile.py:259-299` (hardcoded `.tickets-tracker`, `.bridge_state/bindings*.json`, dups a GHA step); SC7 — `_attestation.py:60-66` (Jira settings) + `:120-135` (git verify).

## Scope
Reconciler git I/O only. NOT the tracker/vendor-backend seam (that is the separate adapter-seam epic). Behavior must stay identical — characterize before/after.

cascade_flag: moves git-call logic out of ~8 modules incl. `_attestation.verify_attested_commit` — sweep every `subprocess.run(["git", ...])` in the reconciler.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One `git_adapter` owns reconciler git ops with explicit repo_root/-C
- [ ] The ~8 modules call the adapter; no direct git subprocess remains in them
- [ ] Binding-store commit-back routes through the adapter; hardcoded paths are named constants
- [ ] `_attestation.py` no longer owns raw git (SC7); git verify uses the adapter (fixes F14 at the seam)
- [ ] Reconciler behavior unchanged (characterization tests before/after)
compiled_state.title: Central git adapter for the reconciler (SC5/SC6/SC7 + F14 seam)
